channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ADC confirms Halliru Jika as governorship candidate for Bauchi 2027 election
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has officially confirmed former Senator Halliru Jika as its governorship candidate for the 2027 Bauchi State election. The confirmation was carried out during an ...

item.title: Central Mindanao leaders praise WestMinCom chief Donald Gumiran for peacebuilding efforts
item.description: Local government officials in Central Mindanao expressed support and appreciation for the special recognition given to Western Mindanao Command chief Lt. Gen. Donald Gumiran by TopNewsAsia Magazine. G...

item.title: Te Maire Martin cited for late contact on Kyle Flanagan in NRL clash
item.description: Te Maire Martin of the New Zealand Warriors has been placed on report early in an NRL match following an incident involving St George Illawarra Dragons half Kyle Flanagan. The alleged offence relates ...

item.title: African parliamentarians visit UCT to review advances in tuberculosis vaccine and diagnostic research
item.description: A delegation of African parliamentarians and global health representatives recently visited the University of Cape Town (UCT), where they were briefed on significant progress in tuberculosis (TB) rese...

item.title: Nigeria receives credit rating upgrade after impact of Dangote refinery strengthens economy
item.description: Nigeria has achieved an important financial milestone after global ratings agency S&P Global Ratings upgraded its sovereign credit rating for the first time in 14 years. The rating was moved from B- t...

item.title: A look at what happened to the players from Pep Guardiola’s first Manchester City line-up in 2016
item.description: The article reflects on Pep Guardiola’s first match as Manchester City manager in August 2016 and examines what has happened to the players from that squad ahead of his reported departure after a deca...

item.title: Filipino musicians and content creators use satire and music to comment on Senate controversies
item.description: The article discusses how several Filipino artists, musicians, and online creators used music, satire, and humor to react to the recent controversies and chaotic incidents involving the Philippine Sen...

item.title: Elvis Presley Interacts With Fans During His 1970 Las Vegas Residency at the International Hotel
item.description: This article revisits a moment from Elvis Presley’s iconic Las Vegas residency during the summer of 1970, focusing on August 26, when he performed two shows at the International Hotel. At this stage i...

item.title: Murkomen’s wife reflects on academic experience under Kithure Kindiki as lecturer and dissertation supervisor
item.description: Gladys Wanjiru, the wife of Interior Cabinet Secretary Kipchumba Murkomen, has shared her personal academic experiences with Deputy President Kithure Kindiki, highlighting his role as both her univers...

item.title: Iqbal Ansari calls for avoiding cow slaughter during Bakrid and urges stronger cow protection measures in India
item.description: Iqbal Ansari, a former litigant in the Babri Masjid land dispute case and a well-known local figure, has made a public appeal ahead of Eid al-Adha (Bakrid), urging members of the Muslim community to a...

item.title: React Challenge Demonstrates Optimistic UI Updates with useOptimistic in React 19
item.description: A new frontend coding challenge published by ReactChallenges introduces developers to React 19’s useOptimistic hook through a practical example involving instant UI updates. The challenge focuses on i...

item.title: How Windows 95 Was Distributed on 28 Floppy Disks Before CD-ROMs Became Standard
item.description: A post published by Vintage Everyday revisits one of the more memorable aspects of mid-1990s personal computing: installing Microsoft Windows 95 using a large set of floppy disks. The article explains...

item.title: McGill researchers develop digital literacy program to help children detect online misinformation
item.description: Researchers at McGill University in Montreal have developed a digital literacy program designed to help elementary school students better identify misinformation online. The initiative comes in respon...

item.title: Julie Burchill Reflects on Marilyn Monroe’s Legacy on the 100th Anniversary of Her Birth
item.description: The article marks the centenary of Marilyn Monroe’s birth and reflects on her enduring cultural influence through the perspective of writer Julie Burchill. Rather than focusing solely on Monroe’s film...

item.title: Charlottesville Plans New Public Artwork Using Melted Robert E. Lee Statue Bronze
item.description: The city of Charlottesville is moving forward with a new phase in the long aftermath of the 2017 white supremacist rally that erupted around a statue of Confederate Gen. Robert E. Lee. After years of ...

item.title: Albanese defends housing and tax reforms amid political backlash at Victorian Labor conference
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has strongly defended his government’s proposed changes to capital gains tax arrangements, negative gearing, and trust structures, presenting the reforms as part of a b...

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson reportedly marry in private ceremony ahead of Bahamas wedding
item.description: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson have reportedly held a private wedding ceremony shortly before their planned official wedding celebration in the Bahamas. According to reports citing a Florida ma...

item.title: Complainant in Joe Sibanyoni extortion case denies R900,000 loan claim
item.description: Thomas Ntuli, the complainant in an ongoing extortion and money laundering case against Mpumalanga taxi boss Joe “Ferrari” Sibanyoni, has strongly denied allegations that he borrowed R900,000 from the...

item.title: Kharge criticises BJP government over repeated fuel price hikes, alleges lack of relief to citizens
item.description: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge has strongly criticised the BJP-led government following the third increase in petrol and diesel prices within eight days. In a post on social media, he alleged t...

item.title: Credit Bank sues Moses Kuria and associated firms over Sh72 million unpaid loan linked to Bomet University projects
item.description: Former Kenyan Cabinet Secretary Moses Kuria, together with associated companies and co-directors, is facing a commercial court case over an alleged unpaid loan amounting to more than Ksh72 million. Th...
